The workspace directory is at `/tmp/mcp-workspace/workspace`. Let me check if there's a `/workspace` directory or if I need to use a fetch script to get the page content since the browser isn't available:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import urllib.request, sys, os

url = "https://sa-forum-aa-1.chats-lab-gui-agent.uk/f/cybersecurity/9/how-to-counter-phishing-attacks"
out = "/tmp/mcp-workspace/workspace/forum_post.html"

req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
    "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36",
    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.5",
    "Accept-Encoding": "identity",
})

try:
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
        html = r.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out), exist_ok=True)
    with open(out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(html)
    print("OK", len(html))
    print(html[:300])
except Exception as e:
    print("ERROR:", e, file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)
